title: The Nineteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## September 29, 2024 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Nineteenth Sunday after Pentecost, placing the church in Ordinary Time following the long season after Pentecost. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would typically include Esther 7:1-6, 9-10; 9:20-22 (or Job 1:1; 2:1-10), Psalm 124, James 5:1-6, and Mark 9:38-50, with themes centering on justice, faithfulness in suffering, and the cost of discipleship. The liturgical color would be green, reflecting the ordinary season of the church year.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

In late September 2024, the Episcopal Church was navigating the aftermath of General Convention held in Louisville, Kentucky in June 2024, which had approved historic measures including authorization of blessings for same-sex couples and continued engagement with LGBTQ+ inclusion. The Diocese of Colorado was experiencing relative stability under Bishop Kym Lucas, who had been serving since 2019, with focus on pastoral ministry and recovery initiatives post-pandemic. No major diocesan controversies or leadership transitions were occurring at this specific moment, though the broader Anglican Communion continued debates over sexuality and biblical interpretation.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the midst of its fall season with typically pleasant early autumn weather, though Colorado was tracking an active wildfire season that had dominated much of the summer and early fall. The Denver mayoral election was underway with Mayor Mike Johnston seeking reelection in a race that would be decided in November, with public safety and homelessness remaining central campaign issues. The Colorado Rockies were finishing a disappointing 2024 MLB season (they would end 59-103), while the Denver Broncos were 2-2 in their NFL season and actively debated at the local level following personnel changes.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

Hurricane Helene made landfall in Florida on September 26, 2024, as a powerful Category 4 hurricane, causing catastrophic damage across the Southeast with significant impacts in Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and surrounding states. Former President Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris were in the final month of the 2024 presidential campaign, with the election scheduled for November 5, 2024; polls showed a tightening race following the September 10 debate. The Federal Reserve was watching inflation data closely and monetary policy remained a significant economic concern for American households, with persistent cost-of-living pressures affecting middle and working-class families.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Israel-Gaza conflict continued with ongoing military operations and humanitarian crisis in Gaza, now in its 12th month since October 7, 2023, with no ceasefire agreement in place and international calls for humanitarian aid intensifying. The Ukraine-Russia war remained active in its third year, with fall military operations underway and continued Western military aid supporting Ukrainian defense efforts. India and Pakistan tensions persisted with longstanding Kashmir disputes and regional security concerns occupying diplomatic attention in South Asia.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The United States in late September 2024 was in a period of high political polarization and anxiety, with the presidential election less than six weeks away and Americans deeply divided along partisan lines. Popular culture was dominated by fall entertainment releases, sports seasons in full swing (NFL, MLB playoffs, college football), and September's conclusion marking the start of autumn—traditionally a moment of reflection and new beginnings. There was an underlying mood of uncertainty about the election outcome and its potential consequences, with many Americans expressing concern about democratic stability and national direction, though some found solace in faith communities and civic engagement.
